parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 17:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Required Fields Present?

### Required Fields Present?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.email }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Slot Free? · ②→ Abort — Malformed Input

### Abort — Malformed Input

Fails fast with a clear message; triggers the error workflow instead of processing bad data.

### Slot Free?

Two-way gate on `={{ $items().length === 0 || !$json.id }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Record Booking · ②→ Propose Alternative Slots

### Record Booking

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Send Confirmation (Gmail) · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Send Confirmation (Gmail)

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

Ports: ①→ Hold Until Reminder Time · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Propose Alternative Slots

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Hold Until Reminder Time

Pauses 1 hours — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Send Reminder (Gmail)

### Send Reminder (Gmail)

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

Ports: ①→ Serialize Run Output (CSV) · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Load Previous Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Load Previous Snapshot

Pulls every page from the Notion database — fine for small datasets; add a filter if the database grows large.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Notion)

### Append Audit Row (Notion)

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#bookings`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Gmail

### Escalate Failure to Gmail

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Booking Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Remind open deals with Notion and Gmail on autopilot

**Pattern:** Reminders & Follow-ups · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Remind open deals with Notion and Gmail on autopilot. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual follow-ups is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Required Fields Present? → Slot Free? → Record Booking → Send Confirmation (Gmail) → Hold Until Reminder Time → Send Reminder (Gmail) → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to reminders & follow-ups without manual effort
- Anyone automating follow-ups in healthcare
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Notion integration token; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Availability is checked *before* booking, not assumed — creating an event unconditionally would double-book the same slot if two requests arrive close together. The confirmation and the later reminder are separate sends because they serve different moments: confirming intent now, and cutting no-shows right before the appointment.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Change the reminder lead time from 1 hour to 24 hours.
2. Simulate two near-simultaneous booking requests for the same slot and see which gets the alternative-slots branch.
3. Add a cancellation path that frees the slot back up.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your follow-ups process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
